definitions.gloss_en: an offensive action directed at an enemy unit or target
definitions.gloss_jp: {敵|てき}{ユニット|ゆにっと}または{対|たい}{象|しょう}に{向|む}けた{攻|こう}{勢|せい}{的|てき}{行|こう}{動|どう}
definitions.explanation_en: The fundamental offensive action in combat. May refer to the act itself, a declared action, or a game mechanic. Covers both ranged and melee contexts unless further qualified.
definitions.implicit_notes: 攻撃 is the general term covering all offensive actions. When specificity is required, 遠距離攻撃 (ranged) or 近接攻撃 (melee) are used.
definitions.orthography_notes: 攻撃: 攻 (attack/assault) + 撃 (strike/hit). Standard Japanese military and game term. Goo dictionary entry is generic. JWC glossary uses 攻撃作戦 (attack operation). Fire Emblem and Famicom Wars use 攻撃 as a core mechanic term. Not WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Universal across all game genres. WH Japan abbreviates it as 攻 (attack stat shorthand) on datasheets, but the full word 攻撃 is entirely generic.

usage_jp: {攻|こう}{撃|げき}する (to attack); {攻|こう}{撃|げき}を{宣|せん}{言|げん}する (declare an attack); {攻|こう}{撃|げき}{力|りょく} (attack stat)
comparative_notes: 攻撃 is the umbrella term. Use 遠距離攻撃 or 近接攻撃 when the attack type must be specified. 攻撃力 (attack power) is the stat form.

examples.japanese: {攻|こう}{撃|げき}は{最|さい}{大|だい}の{防|ぼう}{衛|えい}。
examples.english: Attack is the best form of defense.
